prompt: Two gates in a row — first an OR gate, then an AND gate. What do you think happens if you turn A on?
Two different gates this time! First gate is OR. Will switch A be enough to light the lamp?
feedback: First gate is on — but the lamp is still out! The signal has to travel through both gates. Turn C on too.
OR gate is happy — but the AND gate still needs C! Turn C on next.
The lamp lights up! First the OR gate, then the AND gate — two steps to get there. Now turn C off — what happens?
Yes! First OR, then AND, then lamp. Follow the signal! Now turn C off.
Lamp goes out — the AND gate needs C. The chain broke! First gate, then second gate, then lamp — all must work.
Out! The chain needs both gates. Turn one off and the whole thing stops.
